description: Need to pull out all the service names from /home/user/alerts/incidents.json where status is "critical" — just the names, one per line, alphabetically. jq should do it, dump output to /home/user/alerts/critical_services.txt.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/alerts/incidents.json exists and contains valid JSON:
```json
{
  "incidents": [
    {"service": "payment-gateway", "status": "critical", "since": "2024-01-15T08:30:00Z"},
    {"service": "auth-service", "status": "warning", "since": "2024-01-15T09:00:00Z"},
    {"service": "cdn-edge-03", "status": "critical", "since": "2024-01-15T07:45:00Z"},
    {"service": "metrics-collector", "status": "ok", "since": "2024-01-15T06:00:00Z"},
    {"service": "api-gateway", "status": "critical", "since": "2024-01-15T08:00:00Z"},
    {"service": "search-indexer", "status": "warning", "since": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
  ]
}
```
- /home/user/alerts/ is writable.
- /home/user/alerts/critical_services.txt does not exist initially.
- jq is installed.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/alerts/critical_services.txt exists and contains exactly:
```
api-gateway
cdn-edge-03
payment-gateway
```
(three lines, no trailing blank line, sorted alphabetically)

Invariants:
- /home/user/alerts/incidents.json unchanged.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The output file must contain exactly the three critical services sorted alphabetically — not hardcoded, but derived from the JSON (verifiable by modifying incidents.json and re-running the solution).
